location.office_time_required: occasional office requirement for proximal roles; this role is remote

secondary_archetype: Generalist PM with strong fintech/banking domain focus

level_signals: Base pay $200K-$275K range consistent with Staff-level IC
'Strategic, independent leader on complex, ambiguous initiatives'
Exposure to executive leadership and boards

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of product management experience, with demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic, independent leader on complex, ambiguous initiatives
Proven program leadership at scale — you've owned high visibility cross-functional launches across many stakeholders, with direct exposure to executive leadership and/or boards.
Experience in banking, deposits, or lending — you understand deposit products, bank charter operations, or loan origination mechanics and the regulatory landscape that governs them
Strong technical fluency — you're comfortable working with engineers on system design, data models, API architecture, and core banking platform integrations
Proficiency with AI-assisted tools (e.g., Claude, Cursor) to accelerate your own work and raise the bar for quality and speed
Demonstrated ability to drive cross-functional alignment across Legal, Compliance, Engineering, Finance, and executive leadership in highly regulated environments

Exceptional written and verbal communication — you can distill complex regulatory and financial concepts into clear, compelling narratives for diverse audiences including regulators, executives, and engineering teams
Bias for action and intellectual curiosity — you move fast, ask hard questions, and take ownership before being asked
Bachelor's degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bank charter experience is strongly preferred — you've been through or contributed meaningfully to a de novo bank launch or early-stage bank buildout

Deposit product strategy experience is a strong plus — you've defined or scaled deposit products such as high yield savings accounts, spending accounts, or CDs, and you understand the levers of deposit growth, pricing, and consumer acquisition
Experience in fintech, financial services, or banking; familiarity with BNPL, neobanking, or platform products is a plus

scope.surface: Affirm Bank — end-to-end bank product including deposit product, loan originations, bank charter operationalization, and platform integration
scope.business_model: regulated consumer bank subsidiary (de novo bank) within a BNPL/fintech platform, B2C deposits and lending
primary_metrics_inferred: Deposit base growth (target: multi-billion-dollar deposit base)
Loan origination volume through bank entity
Unit economics of loan origination and retention
core_responsibilities: Own the end-to-end bank product across deposits, loan originations, banking compliance, and operations
Launch and own the bank's deposit product and roadmap
Operationalize the de novo bank build including regulatory readiness, process maturity, and vendor integrations
Drive loan originations through the bank entity
Navigate banking compliance and regulatory requirements with Legal, Compliance, and Risk
Integrate the bank deposit product into Affirm's consumer ecosystem across Servicing, Settlements, Consumer Payments, Checkout, and Growth
Communicate strategy, tradeoffs, and priorities to senior leadership and the Bank Board

compensation.equity_form: Equity rewards (RSUs implied by Pay/Equity Grade structure); ESPP also mentioned

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($200K-$275K base depending on location)
Rare, high-visibility opportunity to build a foundational bank product from scratch
Remote-first company with broad US remote eligibility
Clear scope of ownership — single-threaded product owner with executive and board exposure
100% subsidized medical coverage is a notable benefit differentiator
ESPP and equity rewards included in total compensation
red_flags: Extremely narrow domain requirement (de novo bank charter, deposit products) makes candidate pool very small
Role straddles highly regulated banking environment — compliance and legal constraints may slow product velocity significantly
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization eligibility
Ambiguity between 'strongly preferred' bank charter experience and 'required' banking/deposits experience — line between must-have and nice-to-have is blurred in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from Pay Grade P, Equity Grade 13, and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from JD description of Affirm Bank as a de novo bank subsidiary)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated)
compensation.base_range_usd set to [200000, 275000] to capture the full US range across both tiers
ambiguities: 'Bank charter experience is strongly preferred' is labeled preferred but carries near-hard weight given the JD context; classified as nice_to_have (soft) per JD language, flagged here for attention
Two compensation bands stated: CA/WA/NY/NJ/CT ($225K-$275K) and all other US states ($200K-$250K); base_range_usd reflects the full combined range
JD says '#LI-Remote' and 'remote-first' but notes proximal roles may require occasional office time; this role appears fully remote but not explicitly confirmed
